landmark_structure.justification: Both skip links ('Skip to main content' and 'Skip to navigation') appear as the first focusable elements, appropriate for a sidebar layout where nav is not at the top of the DOM. <main> wraps only page content; sidebar is in <aside> outside <main>. Both <nav> elements are distinctly labelled. <section> is labelled via aria-labelledby.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> 'Projects', <h2> 'All projects' for the section, and <h3> for each project card name — a clean h1→h2→h3 hierarchy with no skipped levels. A comment in the HTML even notes the h3 assumes the section context.

element_choice.justification: Logo uses . Dropdown trigger uses

aria_discipline.justification: No redundant ARIA roles on native elements. Both <nav> elements carry aria-label; the <section> uses aria-labelledby. The <aside> has aria-label='Sidebar' which is acceptable for landmark disambiguation. tabindex='-1' on <main> is correct to make it a skip-link target.

list_semantics.justification: Primary nav items, secondary nav items, and dropdown items are all in <ul> within their respective <nav> elements. Project cards are wrapped in a <ul class='project-cards'> with each card in an <li>. Key-value metadata inside cards correctly uses <dl>.

content_realism.justification: Project names are realistic and distinct (Meridian Platform Redesign, Compass Analytics Integration, Lighthouse Mobile App v2). Statuses, dates, and member counts are varied and plausible. No placeholder content.

expectations_met: Sidebar navigation uses <nav> with a descriptive label ('Primary')
<main> wraps only the projects content area — sidebar is in <aside> outside <main>
Both 'Skip to main content' and 'Skip to navigation' links are the first focusable elements
Two <nav> elements each have a distinct aria-label
Nav items are in a <ul> list
Current page 'Projects' is marked with aria-current='page'
User dropdown uses a <button> with popovertarget (Popover API), not <details>/<summary>
Profile and Preferences are <a> links; Sign Out is a <form><button type='submit'>
Project cards are in a <ul> list
Status badges include visually-hidden 'Status: ' prefix — not conveyed by colour alone
Project card names are <h3>, appropriate given the intermediate <h2> 'All projects' section heading (h1→h2→h3 is correct)
Secondary nav has aria-label='Support and account', clearly distinct from the primary nav
Logo is an containing
additional_observations: The use of <article> for each project card is well-chosen — each card is self-contained and could stand alone.
The <dl> inside each card for last-updated and member-count metadata is an excellent application of definition list semantics for key-value pairs.
The popover target is a <ul> element, which is semantically valid and avoids unnecessary wrapper divs.
The comment on each card noting the h3 assumption shows authorial awareness of heading hierarchy constraints in reusable components.
